title: salix fluviatilis var. exigua
intro: Salix fluviatilis var. exigua, also known as the Dwarf River Willow, is a small shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas, characterized by its small leaves and slender branches. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Salix fluviatilis var. exigua include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Salix fluviatilis var. exigua can be prepared as an infusion or decoction for use in traditional medicine, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
